claim: For all integers n > 1, n is prime if and only if (n-1)! + 1 is divisible by n.
predicate: all((not is_prime(n)) == ((factorial(n-1) + 1) % n != 0) for n in range(2, 501))

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is composite, then (n-1)! is divisible by n.
predicate: all((not is_prime(n)) == (factorial(n-1) % n == 0) for n in range(2, 501))

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime, then (n-1)! ≡ -1 mod n.
predicate: all(is_prime(n) == ((factorial(n-1) + 1) % n == 0) for n in range(2, 501))

claim: For all integers n > 1, (n-1)! ≡ -1 mod n if and only if n is prime.
predicate: all(((factorial(n-1) + 1) % n == 0) == is_prime(n) for n in range(2, 501))

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime, then n divides (n-2)! - 1.
predicate: all(is_prime(n) == ((factorial(n-2) - 1) % n == 0) for n in range(2, 501))

claim: For all integers n > 4, if n is composite, then (n-1)! ≡ 0 mod n.
predicate: all((not is_prime(n) and n > 4) == (factorial(n-1) % n == 0) for n in range(5, 501))

claim: For all integers n > 4, (n-1)! ≡ 0 mod n if and only if n is composite.
predicate: all((factorial(n-1) % n == 0) == (not is_prime(n)) for n in range(5, 501))

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime, then n^2 does not divide (n-1)! + 1.
predicate: all(is_prime(n) == ((factorial(n-1) + 1) % (n**2) != 0) for n in range(2, 501))

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime, then n^3 does not divide (n-1)! + 1.
predicate: all(is_prime(n) == ((factorial(n-1) + 1) % (n**3) != 0) for n in range(2, 501))

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime, then n^k does not divide (n-1)! + 1 for any k > 1.
predicate: all(is_prime(n) == all((factorial(n-1) + 1) % (n**k) != 0 for k in range(2, 10)) for n in range(2, 501))

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime, then (n-1)! ≡ -1 mod n^2.
predicate: all(is_prime(n) == ((factorial(n-1) + 1) % (n**2) == 0) for n in range(2, 501))

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime, then (n-2)! ≡ 1 mod n.
predicate: all(is_prime(n) == ((factorial(n-2) - 1) % n == 0) for n in range(2, 501))

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime, then (n-3)! ≡ (n-1)/2 mod n.
predicate: all(is_prime(n) == ((factorial(n-3) - (n-1)//2) % n == 0) for n in range(2, 501))

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime, then (n-1)! + 1 is divisible by n but not by n^2.
predicate: all(is_prime(n) == (((factorial(n-1) + 1) % n == 0) and ((factorial(n-1) + 1) % (n**2) != 0)) for n in range(2, 501))

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime, then (n-1)! + 1 is not divisible by any prime p < n.
predicate: all(is_prime(n) == all((factorial(n-1) + 1) % p != 0 for p in range(2, n) if is_prime(p)) for n in range(2, 501))

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime, then (n-1)! + 1 is square-free.
predicate: all(is_prime(n) == all(vp(factorial(n-1) + 1, p) <= 1 for p in range(2, factorial(n-1) + 2) if is_prime(p)) for n in range(2, 501))

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime, then (n-1)! + 1 has exactly one prime factor congruent to 1 mod n.
predicate: all(is_prime(n) == (sum(1 for p in range(2, factorial(n-1) + 2) if is_prime(p) and (factorial(n-1) + 1) % p == 0 and p % n == 1) == 1) for n in range(2, 501))

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime, then (n-1)! + 1 is not a power of a prime.
predicate: all(is_prime(n) == (len([p for p in range(2, factorial(n-1) + 2) if is_prime(p) and (factorial(n-1) + 1) % p == 0]) != 1) for n in range(2, 501))

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime, then (n-1)! + 1 is not a perfect square.
predicate: all(is_prime(n) == (round((factorial(n-1) + 1)**0.5)**2 != factorial(n-1) + 1) for n in range(2, 501))

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime, then (n-1)! + 1 is not a perfect cube.
predicate: all(is_prime(n) == (round((factorial(n-1) + 1)**(1/3))**3 != factorial(n-1) + 1) for n in range(2, 501))

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime, then (n-1)! + 1 is not a perfect power.
predicate: all(is_prime(n) == all(round((factorial(n-1) + 1)**(1/k))**k != factorial(n-1) + 1 for k in range(2, 10)) for n in range(2, 501))

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime, then (n-1)! + 1 is not divisible by any prime p ≡ 1 mod n.
predicate: all(is_prime(n) == all((factorial(n-1) + 1) % p != 0 for p in range(2, factorial(n-1) + 2) if is_prime(p) and p % n == 1) for n in range(2, 501))

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime, then (n-1)! + 1 is not divisible by n+1.
predicate: all(is_prime(n) == ((factorial(n-1) + 1) % (n+1) != 0) for n in range(2, 501))

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime, then (n-1)! + 1 is not divisible by n-1.
predicate: all(is_prime(n) == ((factorial(n-1) + 1) % (n-1) != 0) for n in range(2, 501))

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime, then (n-1)! + 1 is not divisible by n+1 or n-1.
predicate: all(is_prime(n) == (((factorial(n-1) + 1) % (n+1) != 0) and ((factorial(n-1) + 1) % (n-1) != 0)) for n in range(2, 501))

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime, then (n-1)! + 1 is not divisible by any prime p < n^2.
predicate: all(is_prime(n) == all((factorial(n-1) + 1) % p != 0 for p in range(2, n**2) if is_prime(p)) for n in range(2, 501))

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime, then (n-1)! + 1 is not divisible by any prime p ≡ n mod n+1.
predicate: all(is_prime(n) == all((factorial(n-1) + 1) % p != 0 for p in range(2, factorial(n-1) + 2) if is_prime(p) and p % (n+1) == n % (n+1)) for n in range(2, 501))

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime, then (n-1)! + 1 is not divisible by any prime p ≡ 1 mod n+1.
predicate: all(is_prime(n) == all((factorial(n-1) + 1) % p != 0 for p in range(2, factorial(n-1) + 2) if is_prime(p) and p % (n+1) == 1) for n in range(2, 501))

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime, then (n-1)! + 1 is not divisible by any prime p ≡ k mod n+1 for k in {1, n}.
predicate: all(is_prime(n) == all((factorial(n-1) + 1) % p != 0 for p in range(2, factorial(n-1) + 2) if is_prime(p) and (p % (n+1) == 1 or p % (n+1) == n % (n+1))) for n in range(2, 501))

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime, then (n-1)! + 1 is not divisible by any prime p ≡ n mod n^2.
predicate: all(is_prime(n) == all((factorial(n-1) + 1) % p != 0 for p in range(2, factorial(n-1) + 2) if is_prime(p) and p % (n**2) == n % (n**2)) for n in range(2, 501))

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime, then (n-1)! + 1 is not divisible by any prime p ≡ 1 mod n^2.
predicate: all(is_prime(n) == all((factorial(n-1) + 1) % p != 0 for p in range(2, factorial(n-1) + 2) if is_prime(p) and p % (n**2) == 1) for n in range(2, 501))
